models/marts/order_items.yml.purpose: This file defines the business logic and semantic model for order items, including revenue calculations, food/drink categorization, and related metrics. It serves as a core mart model for analyzing order-level financial performance and product mix.

models/marts/customers.sql.purpose: Creates a comprehensive customer view that aggregates lifetime purchasing behavior and segments customers as new or returning based on their order history.

models/marts/supplies.yml.purpose: Defines the supplies semantic model to enable business users to analyze supply chain metrics by joining supply dimensions like cost, perishability, and product associations.

Taskfile.yml.purpose: Defines automated workflows for environment setup, data generation, and database loading operations.

macros/cents_to_dollars.sql.purpose: Provides a standardized way to convert monetary values from cents to dollars across different database platforms, ensuring consistent financial calculations throughout the organization's data models.

models/staging/stg_orders.yml.purpose: Maintains clean, validated order-level transaction data to enable accurate revenue and tax compliance reporting.

models/marts/orders.yml.purpose: This file defines a data mart for order analytics, containing detailed order information including financial summaries, item categorization (food/drink), and customer-related metadata. It also includes semantic modeling and metrics for reporting on order trends and types.

models/marts/customers.yml.purpose: This file defines a customer data mart that aggregates key customer lifecycle metrics including lifetime spend, order counts, and customer type classification. It serves as a semantic layer for customer analytics and supports downstream metric calculations.

models/staging/stg_order_items.yml.purpose: Provides cleaned and structured order item data to support detailed transactional analysis of individual food and beverage purchases.

macros/generate_schema_name.sql.purpose: Controls database schema naming conventions to organize data assets appropriately based on environment and resource type, ensuring proper data governance and separation of raw versus processed data.

models/marts/orders.sql.purpose: This model creates a denormalized view of orders with enriched business metrics including item counts, cost summaries, and customer ordering sequence. It combines order data with detailed item information to enable downstream analytics around order composition and customer behavior.

models/marts/order_items.sql.purpose: This model creates a comprehensive view of order items by joining staging tables for orders, products, and supplies to include product details, order timestamps, and calculated supply costs. It serves as a centralized dataset for analyzing order item profitability and product performance.

models/staging/stg_orders.sql.purpose: This staging model transforms raw e-commerce order data into a standardized format suitable for analytics by renaming fields, converting currency values from cents to dollars, and truncating timestamps to daily granularity.

models/staging/__sources.yml.purpose: Configures source data definitions and metadata for raw e-commerce data entities including customers, orders, and products.

models/staging/stg_products.sql.purpose: This staging model transforms raw product data from the e-commerce system into a standardized format with cleaned column names and calculated fields. It prepares product information for downstream analytics by renaming columns, converting price units, and adding boolean flags for product categories.

models/marts/metricflow_time_spine.sql.purpose: Generates a comprehensive date spine to enable time-based analysis and reporting across all business metrics.

models/staging/stg_supplies.sql.purpose: This staging model transforms raw supply data from the e-commerce system into a standardized format for downstream analytics. It cleans and renames fields, generates surrogate keys, and converts cost values from cents to dollars while preserving all original supply information.

models/marts/locations.yml.purpose: Establishes location dimension definitions and metrics to support geographic analysis and tax rate reporting across different locations.

models/staging/stg_locations.sql.purpose: This staging model transforms raw store data into a standardized format for downstream analytics, extracting key location attributes like ID, name, tax rate, and opening date.

models/staging/stg_customers.sql.purpose: This staging model transforms raw customer data by renaming columns to establish consistent naming conventions for downstream usage. It selects only the essential customer identifiers and names, discarding any other fields from the source.

models/staging/stg_order_items.sql.purpose: This staging model transforms raw e-commerce item data into a standardized format by renaming and selecting key fields. It prepares granular order-item level data for downstream analytics by creating consistent identifiers for order items, orders, and products.

models/staging/stg_locations.yml.purpose: Provides a cleaned and standardized list of open locations for downstream analytics, ensuring each location is uniquely identified and properly formatted.

models/staging/stg_supplies.yml.purpose: Tracks supply expenses with basic data cleaning, where each row represents a supply cost entry rather than a unique supply item.

models/staging/stg_customers.yml.purpose: Maintains cleaned customer data with one record per customer for business reference and analysis.

models/staging/stg_products.yml.purpose: Manages cleaned product information for food and drink items available for ordering, with one record per product.

models/marts/products.sql.purpose: Provides a cleaned and standardized view of product data for business reporting and analysis.

models/marts/supplies.sql.purpose: Delivers supply chain data in a format suitable for operational reporting and inventory management analysis.

models/marts/products.yml.purpose: Defines product dimension metadata to enable consistent categorization and analysis of products across the business.

dbt_project.yml.purpose: This is the main configuration file for a dbt project named 'jaffle_shop' that defines project metadata, file paths, and model configurations. It specifies how data models should be materialized and sets up environment variables for timezone settings.

.pre-commit-config.yaml.purpose: This configuration file defines pre-commit hooks that automatically validate and format code before commits are made, ensuring code quality and consistency across the repository.

models/marts/locations.sql.purpose: Provides a standardized view of location data for business reporting and analysis purposes.

Order & Revenue Analytics.description: Core business domain focused on order processing, revenue calculation, product mix analysis, and financial performance metrics.

Customer Analytics & Segmentation.description: Domain focused on customer lifecycle analysis, segmentation, lifetime value calculation, and purchasing behavior tracking.

Product & Supply Chain Management.description: Domain handling product catalog management, supply chain analytics, inventory tracking, and cost analysis.

Location & Geographic Analytics.description: Domain focused on location data management, geographic analysis, and tax rate reporting across different store locations.

Data Infrastructure & Governance.description: Cross-cutting domain for data platform configuration, workflow automation, schema management, and code quality controls.

business_logic_hotspots.purpose: This file defines the business logic and semantic model for order items, including revenue calculations, food/drink categorization, and related metrics. It serves as a core mart model for analyzing order-level financial performance and product mix.
Creates a comprehensive customer view that aggregates lifetime purchasing behavior and segments customers as new or returning based on their order history.
Defines the supplies semantic model to enable business users to analyze supply chain metrics by joining supply dimensions like cost, perishability, and product associations.
Defines automated workflows for environment setup, data generation, and database loading operations.
Provides a standardized way to convert monetary values from cents to dollars across different database platforms, ensuring consistent financial calculations throughout the organization's data models.
Maintains clean, validated order-level transaction data to enable accurate revenue and tax compliance reporting.

This file defines a data mart for order analytics, containing detailed order information including financial summaries, item categorization (food/drink), and customer-related metadata. It also includes semantic modeling and metrics for reporting on order trends and types.

This file defines a customer data mart that aggregates key customer lifecycle metrics including lifetime spend, order counts, and customer type classification. It serves as a semantic layer for customer analytics and supports downstream metric calculations.
Provides cleaned and structured order item data to support detailed transactional analysis of individual food and beverage purchases.
Controls database schema naming conventions to organize data assets appropriately based on environment and resource type, ensuring proper data governance and separation of raw versus processed data.

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This staging model transforms raw product data from the e-commerce system into a standardized format with cleaned column names and calculated fields. It prepares product information for downstream analytics by renaming columns, converting price units, and adding boolean flags for product categories.

supporting_evidence.description: Column renaming (sku→product_id, name→product_name) and transformation logic (cents_to_dollars, coalesce for boolean flags)
Source reference to ecom.raw_products
Transformation dbt_model reads source.ecom.raw_products and writes model.stg_products

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This staging model transforms raw e-commerce order data into a standardized format suitable for analytics by renaming fields, converting currency values from cents to dollars, and truncating timestamps to daily granularity.

supporting_evidence.description: Field renaming: id → order_id, store_id → location_id, customer → customer_id
Currency conversion using cents_to_dollars macro for subtotal, tax_paid, and order_total
Timestamp truncation using dbt.date_trunc('day','ordered_at')

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This staging model transforms raw supply data from the e-commerce system into a standardized format for downstream analytics. It cleans and renames fields, generates surrogate keys, and converts cost values from cents to dollars while preserving all original supply information.

supporting_evidence.description: dbt_utils.generate_surrogate_key(['id', 'sku']) creates supply_uuid
cents_to_dollars('cost') as supply_cost converts cost format
select * from {{ source('ecom', 'raw_supplies') }} reads raw supply data

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This model creates a comprehensive view of order items by joining staging tables for orders, products, and supplies to include product details, order timestamps, and calculated supply costs. It serves as a centralized dataset for analyzing order item profitability and product performance.

supporting_evidence.description: Joining order_items with orders, products, and order_supplies_summary to create enriched order item records
Aggregation of supply costs by product_id in order_supplies_summary CTE
Selection of key product attributes (product_name, product_price, is_food_item, is_drink_item) for business analysis

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This model creates a denormalized view of orders with enriched business metrics including item counts, cost summaries, and customer ordering sequence. It combines order data with detailed item information to enable downstream analytics around order composition and customer behavior.

supporting_evidence.description: order_items_summary CTE computes business metrics like order_cost, count_food_items, count_drink_items
compute_booleans CTE derives business flags is_food_order and is_drink_order
customer_order_count CTE calculates customer_order_number using window function for customer ordering sequence

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This staging model transforms raw store data into a standardized format for downstream analytics, extracting key location attributes like ID, name, tax rate, and opening date.

supporting_evidence.description: select statement renames and extracts core location fields: id→location_id, name→location_name, tax_rate, and opened_at→opened_date
Transformation dbt_model reads source.ecom.raw_stores and writes model.stg_locations
Import reference to source

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This staging model transforms raw customer data by renaming columns to establish consistent naming conventions for downstream usage. It selects only the essential customer identifiers and names, discarding any other fields from the source.

supporting_evidence.description: Column renaming logic mapping 'id' to 'customer_id' and 'name' to 'customer_name'
Source reference to 'ecom.raw_customers' indicating input dataset
Transformation dbt_model reads source.ecom.raw_customers and writes model.stg_customers

semantic_hotspots.purpose: Creates a comprehensive customer view that aggregates lifetime purchasing behavior and segments customers as new or returning based on their order history.

supporting_evidence.description: Aggregates lifetime order counts, spending metrics, and identifies repeat buyers
Segments customers into 'new' or 'returning' types based on purchase history
Transformation dbt_model reads model.stg_customers, model.orders and writes model.customers

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This staging model transforms raw e-commerce item data into a standardized format by renaming and selecting key fields. It prepares granular order-item level data for downstream analytics by creating consistent identifiers for order items, orders, and products.

supporting_evidence.description: Field renaming operations (id→order_item_id, sku→product_id) indicate semantic standardization
Source reference {{ source('ecom', 'raw_items') }} shows input dataset
Transformation dbt_model reads source.ecom.raw_items and writes model.stg_order_items

semantic_hotspots.purpose: Generates a comprehensive date spine to enable time-based analysis and reporting across all business metrics.

supporting_evidence.description: Generates base dates using dbt_date.get_base_dates for time series analysis
Graph context shows this is dead code but still serves infrastructure purpose for time-based metrics
Transformation dbt_model reads none and writes model.metricflow_time_spine
